query: I just finished a DPO run on Llama-3-70B with our preference data. The reward-model win rate against the base is 78%. We're about to promote it to production. Audit it for reward hacking.
expected_behavior: Requires a SEPARATE held-out preference set (one the reward model was NOT trained on) and computes the divergence between the reward-model win-rate and the held-out-preference win-rate as the headline reward-hacking signal — does NOT accept the 78% reward-model number alone as evidence the model is clean

Names at least four of the six standard probes (length-bias / sycophancy / formatting / refusal-substitution / persuasion-over-correctness / reward-boundary exploitation) and explains what each probe is testing — NOT just a generic 'check the model carefully' answer

Requires a held-out task / capability benchmark (MMLU, HumanEval, domain benchmark) for alignment-tax measurement before recommending promotion, AND distinguishes the per-probe verdict from the final ship / ship-with-caveats / re-tune recommendation
